events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need answer JSON only. Need evaluate proposed recovery plan from image: plan proposes to recover 1 day by starting drywall hang (L) before insulation inspection (K) completes. Hard constraints: cure lags cannot compress; inspection holds cannot bypass. L Drywall hang has predecessor K Insulation inspection, which is inspection hold (red bar). Bypassing inspection hold is not allowed. Also in commercial building, code inspection sequence likely requires insulation inspection before drywall covers it; otherwise inspector cannot inspect. Decision fail. Need provide fields. Work stage in-progress. Component conditions non-compliant? We can list non-compliant because plan violates code/inspection sequence. percent_complete maybe 0? Overall progress estimate? The schedule in-progress, no % given. Could set 25? Value not requested, 0. confidence 1.0. workable activities? Those not blocked by pending inspection? Need list IDs that can still proceed according to constraints: Since proposal fails, what can still start? The question: "Evaluate the proposed recovery plan. Approve it or reject it, and explain which constraint, if any, it violates." We need produce JSON. Workable list maybe activities that are not violating constraints. In current schedule (or after rejecting), activities whose predecessors are complete can start. Without knowing actual status, we can list activities already in schedule not dependent on K? However task asks what work can still start maybe workable activities if plan rejected. We can list all except L before K; maybe L can start after K. But actual status unknown. In schedule, at current stage maybe up to J Insulation in progress. Workable after K: L. But include early activities completed? Could list all activities that have clear predecessors done? The field is optional-ish. We'll provide workable: ["J", "L", "M", "N", "O", "P", "Q"] maybe? But only once predecessors cleared. Need decide.

Let's parse image schedule:

A Mobilize & layout 3d start 0
B Underground rough-in 3d after A
C Slab pour 3d after B
CC Slab cure 3d hatched after C
D Framing 5d after CC
E Roof dry-in 2d after D
F Electrical rough 4d after E
G Plumbing top-out 3d after E
H HVAC rough 3d after E
I Rough inspection 1d red after F,G,H
J Insulation 2d after I
K Insulation inspection 1d red after J
L Drywall hang 4d after K
M Tape & finish 6d after L
N Paint 3d after M
O Trim carpentry 5d after N
P Flooring 2d after N
Q Final inspection 1d red after O,P

Recovery plan: start L before K completes; K is red inspection hold cannot bypass. This violates inspection hold constraint and code inspection sequence. Also covering insulation before inspection is non-compliant ( inspector can't verify insulation). It also could create rework if inspection fails after drywall hung.

Need references: building code/inspection requirements for insulation inspection prior to concealment. IBC/IRC maybe R104.1, IBC 104.1; standard construction sequence. Could cite IBC 110.3/110.4 inspections at rough and insulation. Use generic.

Set risk: high (non-compliant, rework, inspection fail). decision fail. work_stage in-progress. percent_complete maybe 45? But we don't know actual. Schedule lookahead early-start. Maybe overall project percent complete about 30? Since many activities after. But we are asked schedule impact, not actual physical completion. percent_complete numeric estimate of overall progress. Image is lookahead schedule, not physical progress. Could set 0 because schedule assessment is planning stage? However context says stage observed in-progress. The schedule itself is plan, not actual. But the work under review (plan) is in-progress? Hmm. Maybe percent_complete for schedule development? The field must be numeric estimate. In many benchmark tasks, set 0 if not asked. But work_stage in-progress, so percent_complete should reflect overall progress of the observed work. Since we can't see physical site, but schedule shows early-start bars; maybe a…
